title: App shell & plumbing
depends: accounts · admin · game · golf-data · leagues · scoring

rows.value: The chrome (root layout, landing page, nav) plus everything cross-cutting: the tRPC client/server wiring, the procedure ladder, the Kysely db singleton, and shared constants/formatters.
Everyone imports from here. Its own six "depends" edges exist only because `_app.ts` must aggregate every domain's router and the layout mounts accounts/leagues chrome.

bigIdea.label: The procedure ladder + one transaction door
bigIdea.line: `publicProcedure` → `protectedProcedure` (throws `UNAUTHORIZED` without a session) → `adminProcedure` (throws `FORBIDDEN` unless `role === "admin"`) — every API call climbs exactly as far as it must.
bigIdea.sub: On the write side the discipline is `withTransaction`: services wrap multi-repository writes in it, repositories are the only layer touching `db`, and the `db.ts` / `db-core.ts` split exists so seed scripts can reuse the same pool outside Next.js.

blocks.title: Components
blocks.intro: Four meaningful units rather than four directories — the tRPC pipeline, the database core, the visible chrome, and the shared vocabulary every page borrows.

cards.name: tRPC plumbing
cards.purpose: Context creation, the three-step procedure ladder, the root `appRouter` merging 11 domain routers, the `/api/trpc` HTTP handler, and both consumption paths — browser hooks and an in-process server caller.

cards.name: db core
cards.purpose: The singleton Kysely instance over a `pg.Pool` (camelCase plugin, dev-mode `globalThis` cache) and `withTransaction`, split into a guarded and an unguarded module.

cards.name: app chrome
cards.purpose: Root layout (fonts, metadata, `Providers` + `SessionRefresh` + `NavBar`), the signed-out landing page, and the nav bar with its mobile menu.

cards.name: shared utils
cards.purpose: The league-wide constant `GLOBAL_LEAGUE_ID` and the domain-aware formatters (`formatCurrency`, `isMissedCut`, …) used by ~25 files across every UI domain.

blocks.title: Internal architecture
blocks.intro: The request path. Two entry doors — client components speak HTTP to `/api/trpc`, server components call the router in-process via `serverTRPC` — and both converge on `createTRPCContext`, the procedure ladder, and the same router → service → repository → Postgres stack.

diagram.d2:
direction: right
Browser: { class: actor }
"Server page": { class: actor }
"route.ts /api/trpc"
serverTRPC
createTRPCContext
"procedure ladder": { class: changed }
"domain router"
service
repository
Postgres: { class: store }
Browser -> "route.ts /api/trpc": httpBatchStreamLink
"Server page" -> serverTRPC: direct caller
"route.ts /api/trpc" -> createTRPCContext
serverTRPC -> createTRPCContext
createTRPCContext -> "procedure ladder": session
"procedure ladder" -> "domain router"
"domain router" -> service
service -> repository: withTransaction
repository -> Postgres: Kysely
diagram.caption: Both doors share one context: `createTRPCContext` resolves the Better Auth session from request headers, then the ladder decides how far the call may climb. Server pages never pay an HTTP round-trip — `serverTRPC` uses `createCallerFactory(appRouter)`.

blocks.callout: Everything below the ladder belongs to the neighbor domains — web-shell owns the pipe, not the water.

components.name: tRPC plumbing
detail: `server/trpc.ts` is the smallest and most load-bearing file in the repo. `createTRPCContext` calls `auth.api.getSession` (Better Auth, from the [accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html) domain) against the incoming headers and yields `{ session, userId }`. `initTRPC` is configured once with the `superjson` transformer — matching the transformer on the client link, so `Date`/`Decimal` values survive the wire.

The ladder is two middleware steps: `protectedProcedure` throws `UNAUTHORIZED` when `ctx.userId` is null (and narrows its type to `string` for everything downstream), and `adminProcedure` extends it, throwing `FORBIDDEN` unless the session user's `role` is `"admin"`. Every one of the 11 domain routers builds its procedures from one of these three rungs.

`routers/_app.ts` is pure aggregation: it merges `auth`, `health`, `tournament`, `golfer`, `pick`, `dashboard`, `league`, `leaderboard`, `admin`, `earningsAdjustment`, and `user` into `appRouter` and exports the `AppRouter` type — the single type that powers end-to-end inference in every client component. Notably there is **no payments router**: PayPal talks over plain API routes, so [payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html) is absent from this file.

Consumption is two-sided. `lib/trpc/client.tsx` (client) builds a TanStack Query `QueryClient` (30s `staleTime`, browser-singleton) and a tRPC client with a single `httpBatchStreamLink` to `/api/trpc`; `createTRPCContext<AppRouter>()` from `@trpc/tanstack-react-query` yields the `useTRPC` hook. `lib/trpc/server.ts` (server) skips HTTP entirely: `createCallerFactory(appRouter)` plus a fresh context gives `serverTRPC()`, the in-process caller used by 27 server pages and the `NavBar`.

diagrams.d2:
direction: right
publicProcedure -> protectedProcedure: "userId present, else UNAUTHORIZED"
protectedProcedure -> adminProcedure: "role is admin, else FORBIDDEN"
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  publicProcedure -->|userId present else UNAUTHORIZED| protectedProcedure
  protectedProcedure -->|role is admin else FORBIDDEN| adminProcedure
diagrams.caption: Each rung is a middleware `use()` on the previous one — auth checks live here once, never in routers.

files.name: server/trpc.ts
files.desc: `createTRPCContext` (session + userId), `initTRPC` with superjson, and the three procedure builders.

files.name: server/routers/_app.ts
files.desc: Root router — merges the 11 domain routers; exports `appRouter` + the `AppRouter` type.

files.name: app/api/trpc/[trpc]/route.ts
files.desc: The one HTTP endpoint: `fetchRequestHandler` at `/api/trpc`, exported as both `GET` and `POST`.

files.name: lib/trpc/server.ts
files.desc: Server side — `serverTRPC()`, an in-process caller built from `createCallerFactory(appRouter)`.

exports.name: publicProcedure
exports.desc: Bottom rung — no auth; used by landing/leaderboard/tournament reads.

exports.name: protectedProcedure
exports.desc: Throws `UNAUTHORIZED` without a session; narrows `ctx.userId` to `string`.

exports.name: adminProcedure
exports.desc: Extends protected; throws `FORBIDDEN` unless session role is `admin`.

exports.name: createTRPCContext
exports.desc: Resolves the Better Auth session from `headers()` into `{ session, userId }`.

exports.name: appRouter
exports.desc: The composed API. `AppRouter` (its type) is what every client imports for inference.

exports.name: serverTRPC
exports.desc: Async factory returning a direct caller — server components' door, no HTTP.

exports.name: useTRPC
exports.desc: The TanStack-Query-integrated hook client components consume.

exports.name: TRPCReactProvider
exports.desc: Mounts QueryClientProvider + TRPCProvider; wired in by `Providers`.

connections.body: All 11 domain routers import their procedure rungs from `server/trpc.ts` — [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html) (pick, dashboard), [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html) (tournament, golfer), [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html), [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html) (leaderboard, earningsAdjustment), [accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html) (auth, user), [admin](../domain-admin/domain-admin.html) (admin, health).
27 server pages call `serverTRPC()`; 21 client components call `useTRPC()` — every data fetch in the app passes through this component.
`createTRPCContext` calls `auth.api.getSession` from [accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html)' `server/auth.ts` — web-shell's only genuine runtime dependency on a neighbor.
`_app.ts` imports every domain's router — the one place the dependency arrow points *out* of web-shell toward everyone else.

components.name: db core
detail: `lib/db-core.ts` builds the singleton `Kysely<DB>` over a `pg.Pool` on `DATABASE_URL`, with `CamelCasePlugin` (snake_case columns → camelCase properties) and the generated types from `@/generated/kysely/types`. In non-production it caches the instance on `globalThis` so Next.js hot reloads don't leak pools. The pool registers a swallow-all `error` handler — when Vercel freezes a serverless function, idle connections get killed, and without the handler that surfaces as an uncaught exception.

`withTransaction(fn)` is the entire transaction API: it runs `fn` inside `db.transaction().execute()` and hands the transaction executor to the callback. The convention (enforced by review, visible in every service) is that repository functions accept an optional `executor: Kysely<DB> = db` parameter, so a service can pass the transaction through multiple repository calls atomically.

`lib/db.ts` is a two-line gate: `import "server-only"` then re-export `{ db, withTransaction }` from `db-core`. App code imports `@/lib/db` and gets a build error if the import ever leaks into a client bundle; the 14 seed scripts under `prisma/seed/` import `@/lib/db-core` directly because they run outside Next.js where `server-only` would throw.

files.name: lib/db-core.ts
files.desc: Pool + Kysely singleton + `withTransaction`. No `server-only` guard — importable by seed scripts.

files.name: lib/db.ts
files.desc: The guarded door: `server-only` + re-export. What app code should import.

exports.name: db
exports.desc: The `Kysely<DB>` singleton. Only repositories (and two sanctioned exceptions) touch it.

exports.name: withTransaction
exports.desc: `(fn: (tx: Kysely<DB>) => Promise<T>) => Promise<T>` — services' only transaction primitive.

connections.body: Every repository in every domain imports `db`; every multi-write service imports `withTransaction` (and only that — services never touch `db` directly, per the layering rule).

`prisma/seed/*` (14 scripts) import `db-core` directly — and so do the repositories those scripts reuse (`sync`, `upserts`, `bot-picks`, `earnings`, `pick-lock`, `cron-log`, …), which deliberately import `@/lib/db-core` instead of `@/lib/db` to stay usable outside Next.js.

Two files outside the repository layer import `db`: [accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html)' `server/auth.ts` (Better Auth needs the pool for its adapter) and [admin](../domain-admin/domain-admin.html)'s `routers/health.ts` (the health check pings the DB from the router).

components.name: app chrome
detail: `app/layout.tsx` (`RootLayout`) loads two Google fonts as CSS variables (Literata + Hanken Grotesk), declares the site-wide `metadata` (title template, Open Graph, Twitter card), imports the `flag-icons` stylesheet, and wraps every page in `Providers` → `SessionRefresh` → `NavBar` → children. `Providers` is a one-liner that mounts `TRPCReactProvider`; `SessionRefresh` comes from [accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html).

`NavBar` is an **async server component**: it calls `serverTRPC()` on every render, checks `auth.me()`, and returns `null` when signed out — which is why the landing page renders its own header with a Sign In link. Signed in, it fetches `league.myLeagues()`, resolves the active league via `getActiveLeagueId` (from [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html)), and renders the `LeagueSwitcher`, desktop links (Dashboard / Season / Standings / Leagues), and the same links again inside `MobileMenuToggle`.

`MobileMenuToggle` is the only client component here: a hamburger button + slide-out panel with two close behaviors (any link/button click inside the menu, any click outside it), tracked with refs and `aria-expanded`.

`app/page.tsx` (`Home`) is the signed-out landing page — it redirects authenticated users to `/dashboard`, then fetches `tournament.current()` and the top-10 of `leaderboard.global()` (both `.catch`-guarded so an empty DB still renders) and lays out hero, how-it-works, live leaderboard teaser, league pitch, and footer.

files.name: app/page.tsx
files.desc: `Home` — signed-out landing page; redirects members to `/dashboard`; shows live tournament + global top 10.

files.name: components/layout/nav-bar.tsx
files.desc: Async server component; `null` when signed out; league switcher + nav links + mobile menu.

files.name: components/layout/mobile-menu-toggle.tsx
files.desc: Client hamburger/panel with outside-click and link-click close handling.

files.name: components/providers.tsx
files.desc: Client boundary that mounts `TRPCReactProvider` for the whole tree.

exports.name: RootLayout
exports.desc: Default export of `layout.tsx` — the app-wide HTML shell.

exports.name: metadata
exports.desc: Site-wide title template + OG/Twitter cards (landing page overrides its own).

exports.name: Home
exports.desc: Default export of `page.tsx` — the marketing/landing page.

exports.name: NavBar
exports.desc: The signed-in navigation chrome.

exports.name: MobileMenuToggle
exports.desc: Mobile menu state + a11y wiring.

exports.name: Providers
exports.desc: Client-side provider stack (currently just tRPC/React Query).

connections.body: [accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html): `SessionRefresh` in the layout, `SignOutButton` in the nav, `auth.me()` for the session check.
[golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)'s `tournament.current()` and [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html)'s `leaderboard.global()` for the landing page's live panels.

components.name: shared utils
detail: `lib/constants.ts` is a single well-known ID: `GLOBAL_LEAGUE_ID = "ppgl-global"`, the league every user is auto-enrolled in. The [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html) leaderboard service and [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html) league service branch on it, and UI in leagues/admin special-cases it.

`lib/format.ts` is where display rules encode game rules. `formatCurrency` renders whole-dollar USD; `formatPosition` does ordinal suffixes (with the 11th/12th/13th trap handled); `formatScore` renders golf score-to-par (`E`, `+3`, `-5`, `–` for null). `formatPurse` prefers the actual purse and falls back to `~$X` for estimates. `formatDate` pins `America/New_York` so tournament dates don't shift per viewer timezone.

`isMissedCut` is the most game-aware helper: a pick is a missed cut only when it earned exactly $0 with no real finish position **and** is `LOCKED` — and never while the tournament is `IN_PROGRESS`, because in round 1 everyone has $0 and a null position; the cut simply hasn't happened yet.

files.name: lib/constants.ts
files.desc: `GLOBAL_LEAGUE_ID` — the auto-enroll global league's well-known ID.

files.name: lib/format.ts
files.desc: Currency / position / score / purse / date formatters + the `isMissedCut` rule.

exports.name: GLOBAL_LEAGUE_ID
exports.desc: `"ppgl-global"` — branch point for global-league behavior in services and UI.

exports.name: formatCurrency
exports.desc: Whole-dollar `en-US` USD — earnings everywhere.

exports.name: formatPosition
exports.desc: `1 → 1st`, `13 → 13th` — finish positions.

exports.name: isMissedCut
exports.desc: $0 + no finish + `LOCKED`, and only on non-live tournaments.

exports.name: formatPurse
exports.desc: Actual purse, else `~` + estimated, else null.

exports.name: formatDate
exports.desc: Short month/day, pinned to `America/New_York`.

connections.body: ~25 files import `format.ts` — dashboard and planner in [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html), standings/earnings in [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html), tournament/golfer pages in [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html), league pages in [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html), and most of [admin](../domain-admin/domain-admin.html).
`GLOBAL_LEAGUE_ID` is read by the leaderboard and league services plus the leagues and admin-tournament UIs.

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: None — and that's the point. web-shell is pure plumbing: it owns the connection to the database, not any table in it.

rows.name: (none)
rows.desc: No Prisma models attribute here. The Kysely `db` singleton and `withTransaction` are owned here, but every table they touch belongs to a neighbor domain.

blocks.note: If you're looking for a model, start from the feature domain that owns it — e.g. picks in [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html), tournaments in [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html).

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: web-shell's surface is the contract every other domain builds on: the procedure rungs, the two tRPC doors, the transaction primitive, and the shared formatters.

exposes.note: Unauthenticated rung — landing page reads, health.
Session-required rung; the workhorse for all member features.
The composed API; its `AppRouter` type drives end-to-end inference.
The single HTTP endpoint (also `GET`), batched + streamed + superjson.
In-process caller — server components skip HTTP entirely.
Services' only way to make multi-repository writes atomic.
Kysely singleton — repositories only (plus `auth.ts` and `health.ts` by design).
Representative of the `format.ts` family used across every UI domain.
Well-known global-league ID consumed by scoring/leagues services.

blocks.note: The depends list is an inversion artifact, not real coupling: `_app.ts` must import every domain's router to compose `appRouter`, and the layout/nav mount accounts + leagues chrome. Conceptually the arrows all point the other way — every domain builds on web-shell. [payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html) is the one absent neighbor: it has no tRPC router (PayPal uses plain API routes), so web-shell never touches it.
